user_question: Wait, I heard Apple basically bought Severance outright from its production company — is that true?

content.content:
{"title": "List of mergers and acquisitions by Apple", "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_mergers_and_acquisitions_by_Apple", "lead": "Apple Inc. is an American multinational corporation that designs and manufactures consumer electronics and software products. It was established in Cupertino, California, on April 1, 1976, by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne, and was incorporated on January 3, 1977. The company's hardware products include the Macintosh line of personal computers, the iPod line of portable media players, the iPad line of tablets, the iPhone line of smartphones, the Apple TV line of digital media players, and the Apple Watch line of smartwatches. Apple's software products include the macOS, iOS, iPadOS, tvOS, and watchOS operating systems, the iTunes media player, the Safari web browser, and the iLife suite of multimedia and creativity software. As of May 2026, Apple is publicly known to have acquired more than 100 companies. The actual number of acquisitions is possibly larger as Apple does not reveal the majority of its acquisitions unless discovered by the press. Apple has cofounded two half-equity partnerships and purchased equity stakes in three preexisting companies. Apple has not released the financial details for the majority of its mergers and acquisitions.
Apple has tended to acquire small companies that can be easily integrated into existing company projects. For instance, Apple acquired Emagic and its professional music software, Logic Pro, in 2002. The acquisition was incorporated in the creation of the digital audio workstation software GarageBand, an integral part of the iLife software suite, and now one of the leading digital audio workstations on iOS and macOS. Apple generally discontinues the existing businesses of acquired companies.
The company made its first acquisition on March 2, 1988, with its purchase of Network Innovations. In 2013, Apple acquired thirteen companies. Apple's largest acquisition was that of Beats Electronics in August 2014 for $3 billion.
In early-May 2019, Apple CEO Tim Cook said to CNBC that Apple acquires a company every two to three weeks on average, having acquired 20 to 25 companies in the past six months alone. In 2021, Cook told shareholders that Apple had "acquired nearly 100 companies" in the preceding six years.", "error": null}

{"title": "Severance (TV series)", "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Severance_(TV_series)", "lead": "Severance is an American science fiction psychological thriller television series created by Dan Erickson, and executive produced and primarily directed by Ben Stiller. It stars Adam Scott, Zach Cherry, Britt Lower, Tramell Tillman, Jen Tullock, Dichen Lachman, Michael Chernus, John Turturro, Christopher Walken, Patricia Arquette, and Sarah Bock. The series follows employees at Lumon Industries, a biotechnology corporation, that have undergone \"severance\"\u2014a procedure that splits a person's memories between work and their personal life. This creates two separate identities for employees: the \"innie\", who has no knowledge of the outside world, and the \"outie\", who lives their life outside without any knowledge of their job.\nErickson and Stiller first developed Severance in 2015, with the series being greenlit by Apple TV in 2019, with Scott attached. The cast for the first season was rounded out by December 2020, and the cast for the second season was announced in October 2022. Principal photography for the series has taken place in New York, New Jersey and Newfoundland. Its first season aired from February 18 until April 8, 2022, and its second season from January 17 to March 21, 2025. Severance has been renewed for a third season.  In February 2026, it was reported that Apple TV had acquired the intellectual property and all rights to the series from Fifth Season, with Apple Studios to become the production company for the series starting with the third season.\nSeverance received acclaim for its cinematography, direction, production design, musical score, story, and performances. Its accolades include 41 Primetime Emmy Award nominations, winning for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Drama Series for Lower and Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Drama Series for Tillman. Scott was nominated for a Primetime Emmy Award, two Television Critics Association Awards, and a Golden Globe Award, while Stiller and Erickson received Emmy nominations for directing and writing.", "error": null}

user_question: Remind me — Foundation is based on those old Asimov books, right? Who adapted it for TV?

messages.content: Remind me — Foundation is based on those old Asimov books, right? Who adapted it for TV?

content.text:
<think>
The user is asking about the Foundation TV series and whether it's based on Isaac Asimov's Foundation books, and who adapted it for television. 

I have knowledge about this from my training data. Foundation is indeed based on Isaac Asimov's Foundation series of science fiction novels. The TV adaptation began airing on Apple TV+ in 2021, and it was created/adapted by David S. Goyer.

Since this is asking about a TV adaptation that exists and I'm confident in this information, I should be able to answer this from my own knowledge. The show premiered in 2021 and is still ongoing as of my knowledge cutoff in February 2025. The current date is May 2026, so there may be updates, but the basic fact about who created/adapted it (David S. Goyer) should still be accurate.

I feel confident enough to answer this without using the search tool.
</think>

Yes, exactly! **Foundation** is based on Isaac Asimov's classic Foundation series of science fiction novels from the 1950s.

The TV adaptation was created and adapted by **David S. Goyer**. The series premiered on Apple TV+ in 2021 and is produced by Skydance Television. Goyer serves as the showrunner and executive producer, bringing Asimov's ambitious story of a galactic empire's fall and the mathematical science of psychohistory to the screen.

Yes, exactly! **Foundation** is based on Isaac Asimov's classic Foundation series of science fiction novels from the 1950s.

The TV adaptation was created and adapted by **David S. Goyer**. The series premiered on Apple TV+ in 2021 and is produced by Skydance Television. Goyer serves as the showrunner and executive producer, bringing Asimov's ambitious story of a galactic empire's fall and the mathematical science of psychohistory to the screen.
golden_answer: Yes, Foundation is based on the Foundation series of stories by Isaac Asimov. It was developed for Apple TV+ by David S. Goyer and Josh Friedman, and premiered in September 2021.
judge_reasoning: The agent correctly identifies Foundation as based on Asimov's books and names David S. Goyer as the adapter, but omits Josh Friedman, who co-developed the series alongside Goyer per the golden answer.

content.content: {"title": "Foundation (TV series)", "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundation_(TV_series)", "lead": "Foundation is an American science fiction television series created by David S. Goyer and Josh Friedman for Apple TV+, based on the Foundation series of stories by Isaac Asimov. It features an ensemble cast led by Jared Harris, Lee Pace, Lou Llobell and Leah Harvey. The series premiered on September 24, 2021. The second and third seasons were released in July 2023 and July 2025, respectively. In September 2025, the series was renewed for a fourth season.\n\n", "error": null}

{"title": "List of Apple TV original programming", "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Apple_TV_original_programming", "lead": "Apple Inc. has produced and distributed its own original content since 2016. Since 2019, it has distributed shows and movies on its Apple TV streaming service.
The first television show produced by Apple was Planet of the Apps, a 2016 reality competition series. Their second, released in late 2017, was Carpool Karaoke: The Series, based on the popular recurring segment from The Late Late Show with James Corden. 
In 2017, Apple appointed Jamie Erlicht and Zack Van Amburg to head a new video unit, then announced that it would produce original scripted programming, starting with straight-to-series orders for two television shows: a reboot of the anthology series Amazing Stories, by Steven Spielberg, and The Morning Show, a drama series starring Jennifer Aniston and Reese Witherspoon. 
That year, reports emerged that Apple planned to spend about $1 billion on original programming through 2018, then that the company planned to spend $4.2 billion on original programming by 2022. In August 2019, it was reported that Apple had already spent over $6 billion on original programming.
On March 25, 2019, Apple announced a new slate of original programming and released a short film, Peanuts in Space: Secrets of Apollo 10. The company also announced a new streaming service, Apple TV+, which launched on November 1, 2019, in over 100 countries through the Apple TV app. The service was renamed Apple TV in October 2025.", "error": null}

The third season wrapped in July 2025, and the renewal announcement came just two months later, so Apple clearly sees value in continuing the story.

content.content: {"title": "Severance (TV series)", "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Severance_(TV_series)", "lead": "Severance is an American science fiction psychological thriller television series created by Dan Erickson, and executive produced and primarily directed by Ben Stiller. It stars Adam Scott, Zach Cherry, Britt Lower, Tramell Tillman, Jen Tullock, Dichen Lachman, Michael Chernus, John Turturro, Christopher Walken, Patricia Arquette, and Sarah Bock. The series follows employees at Lumon Industries, a biotechnology corporation, that have undergone \"severance\"\u2014a procedure that splits a person's memories between work and their personal life. This creates two separate identities for employees: the \"innie\", who has no knowledge of the outside world, and the \"outie\", who lives their life outside without any knowledge of their job.\nErickson and Stiller first developed Severance in 2015, with the series being greenlit by Apple TV in 2019, with Scott attached. The cast for the first season was rounded out by December 2020, and the cast for the second season was announced in October 2022. Principal photography for the series has taken place in New York, New Jersey and Newfoundland. Its first season aired from February 18 until April 8, 2022, and its second season from January 17 to March 21, 2025. Severance has been renewed for a third season.  In February 2026, it was reported that Apple TV had acquired the intellectual property and all rights to the series from Fifth Season, with Apple Studios to become the production company for the series starting with the third season.\nSeverance received acclaim for its cinematography, direction, production design, musical score, story, and performances. Its accolades include 41 Primetime Emmy Award nominations, winning for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Drama Series for Lower and Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Drama Series for Tillman. Scott was nominated for a Primetime Emmy Award, two Television Critics Association Awards, and a Golden Globe Award, while Stiller and Erickson received Emmy nominations for directing and writing.", "error": null}

{"title": "Adam Scott", "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adam_Scott", "lead": "Adam Paul Scott (born April 3, 1973) is an American actor and comedian. He is best known for his role as Ben Wyatt in the NBC sitcom Parks and Recreation (2010\u20132015), for which he was twice nominated for a Critics' Choice Television Award, as well as Mark Scout in the Apple TV+ sci-fi thriller series Severance, for which he was nominated for four Primetime Emmy Awards \u2013 two for acting and two for producing \u2013 and two Golden Globe Awards. \nScott has also appeared in the films The Aviator (2004), Step Brothers (2008) and Flower (2017), as well as the Starz sitcom Party Down (2009\u20132010; 2023), the HBO drama series Big Little Lies (2017\u20132019), and the NBC comedy series The Good Place (2016\u20132018).", "error": null}

content.text:

Based on the search results, I found important information:

1. From the Severance Wikipedia page: "Its accolades include 41 Primetime Emmy Award nominations, winning for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Drama Series for Lower and Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Drama Series for Tillman."

2. From the Adam Scott Wikipedia page: He "was nominated for four Primetime Emmy Awards – two for acting and two for producing – and two Golden Globe Awards" for Severance.

So the answer is: Adam Scott was nominated for Emmy Awards for Severance (4 nominations total - 2 for acting and 2 for producing) but did NOT win. The show did win two Emmy Awards - one for Britt Lower (Outstanding Lead Actress in a Drama Series) and one for Tramell Tillman (Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Drama Series).
</thinking>

Based on the information available, **Adam Scott has not won an Emmy for Severance yet**. However, he has received recognition for his work on the show—he has been nominated for **four Primetime Emmy Awards** for the series (two for acting and two for producing), as well as two Golden Globe nominations.

That said, **Severance has been very successful at the Emmys overall**. The show has received **41 Primetime Emmy Award nominations** and has won at least two Emmy Awards:
- **Britt Lower** won for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Drama Series
- **Tramell Tillman** won for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Drama Series

Both Scott and creator Dan Erickson have received Emmy nominations for their work as well—Scott for his acting, and Erickson for writing. The show has also been renewed for a third season, which speaks to its critical acclaim and success.
